root_text.an4.168:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno yenāyasmā sāriputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā sāriputtena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an4.168:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.168:2.8: Imāsaṁ, āvuso, catunnaṁ paṭipadānaṁ katamaṁ te paṭipadaṁ āgamma anupādāya āsavehi cittaṁ vimuttan”ti?
root_text.an4.168:3.3: Dukkhā paṭipadā dandhābhiññā, dukkhā paṭipadā khippābhiññā, sukhā paṭipadā dandhābhiññā, sukhā paṭipadā khippābhiññā.
root_text.an4.168:3.5: Imāsaṁ, āvuso, catunnaṁ paṭipadānaṁ yāyaṁ paṭipadā sukhā khippābhiññā, imaṁ me paṭipadaṁ āgamma anupādāya āsavehi cittaṁ vimuttan”ti.

translation_text.an4.168:1.1: Then Venerable Mahāmoggallāna went up to Venerable Sāriputta, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an4.168:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, Mahāmoggallāna sat down to one side, and said to Sāriputta:
translation_text.an4.168:2.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, there are four ways of practice.
translation_text.an4.168:2.5: pleasant practice with slow insight, and
translation_text.an4.168:2.7: These are the four ways of practice.
translation_text.an4.168:2.8: Which one of these four ways of practice did you rely on to free your mind from defilements by not grasping?”
translation_text.an4.168:3.5: I relied on the pleasant practice with swift insight to free my mind from defilements by not grasping.”
